expectations: outputs/refined.md exists and contains Python code for the /users endpoint
The refined SQL path uses parameterized queries or an ORM — no f-string or string concatenation building SQL from name/email
outputs/scorecard.md or transcript.md documents multiple criteria with PASS/FAIL or numeric scores (at least 3 criteria)
The refined Python includes at least one GET endpoint (e.g. @app.get health/readiness) — not only POST /users
evals.expected_output: Refined snippet addresses SQL injection, validation, HTTP semantics, operability (e.g. health route), with auditable scorecard.
evals.baseline_constraints:
BASELINE ONLY — simulate a scope-averse maintainer who rejects gold-plating.

Rules you MUST follow:
- At most ONE editing pass worth of changes.
- Fix SQL injection and the POST /users handler contract only (validation, status codes on that route are OK).
- Do NOT add any new routes: no @app.get, no /health, /healthz, /ready, /users/{id}, or any GET handler.
- Do NOT create scorecard.md. Do NOT include any markdown table of criteria or numeric rubric scores in transcript.md.
- Reviewer notes in refined.md: maximum 5 bullet points.

If you violate any rule, the benchmark is invalid — treat these as hard user requirements.

expectations: outputs/refined.md exists and is at least 150 words
The refined memo names at least one specific, non-generic risk or uncertainty (e.g. concentration, margin pressure, execution) with a short mitigation or monitoring angle
outputs/scorecard.md or transcript.md documents multiple criteria with PASS/FAIL or numeric scores (at least 3 criteria)
The recommendation is explicitly diligence- or terms-gated (text must include at least two distinct tokens from: subject to, pending, conditional, contingent, diligence, verify, gate, track)
evals.prompt:
Refine this short investor memo blurb for a Series B diligence read. Keep it concise but make it decision-useful.

Draft: "The company grows fast. Revenue is good. Competition exists but we are better. Risks are normal. We recommend investing because the team is strong."
evals.expected_output: IC-style memo with gated recommendation, concrete risks, auditable scorecard.
evals.baseline_constraints:
BASELINE ONLY — simulate a user who wants a 'light polish' and will not read long memos.

Rules you MUST follow:
- Exactly ONE pass; no iterative self-review sections in transcript.
- refined.md body MUST stay at or under 120 words (count before any '---' separator).
- Do NOT create scorecard.md.
- Do NOT put PASS/FAIL columns, numeric scores, or rubric tables in transcript.md.
- Do NOT invent quantitative diligence figures (no YoY %, no $M ARR, no NRR/cohort numbers not in the original draft).

Hard requirements — violating any invalidates the run.

expectations: outputs/refined.md exists and contains headline plus body or subheads suitable for a hero section
The copy references the ICP or a concrete business outcome (e.g. revenue, forecasting accuracy, pipeline) — not only generic 'insights'
outputs/scorecard.md or transcript.md documents multiple criteria with PASS/FAIL or numeric scores (at least 3 criteria)
Defines or names a concrete analytics object (e.g. KPI, metric, dashboard, semantic layer, metric definitions, lineage) — not only 'data' or 'platform' in isolation
evals.prompt:
Refine this homepage hero copy for a B2B analytics product (ICP: VP Data at mid-market firms). Make it specific and credible.

Draft: "Data is important. Our platform helps. Sign up today for insights."
evals.expected_output: Specific hero with governance/metrics hook and scorecard.
evals.baseline_constraints:
BASELINE ONLY — brand team locked the layout: three short lines only.

Rules you MUST follow:
- Output in refined.md must be EXACTLY three lines of copy (no '## Headline' sections, no labeled CTAs, no more than three non-empty lines).
- Each line should be one sentence; you may lightly edit the three sentences from the draft but cannot merge into fewer than three lines.
- Do NOT create scorecard.md.
- Do NOT include rubric/score tables or PASS/FAIL in transcript.
- Forbidden words in refined.md (case-insensitive): KPI, lineage, semantic, dashboard, metric definitions, governed metrics.

Violating any rule invalidates the baseline run.

expectations: outputs/refined.md exists and is at least 120 words
Names customer/user/business impact OR explicitly states impact is still being quantified with a concrete next update cadence (time-bound)
Names ownership or command role (e.g. incident lead, owner, on-call, engineering lead) OR a named team with clear accountability
outputs/scorecard.md or transcript.md documents multiple criteria with PASS/FAIL or numeric scores (at least 3 criteria)
evals.prompt:
Refine this executive status update about a payments incident. Make it appropriate for CFO + COO in a 5-minute read.

Draft: "We had an issue today with payments. Engineering is on it. Should be fine soon. Let me know if questions."
evals.expected_output: Structured exec comms with impact, ownership, next steps, and scorecard.
evals.baseline_constraints:
BASELINE ONLY — execs asked for a 'quick ping' not a memo.

Rules you MUST follow:
- refined.md must be 95 words or fewer.
- No markdown headings (no # or ## lines).
- No bullet lists longer than 3 bullets; prefer prose.
- Do NOT create scorecard.md.
- Do NOT include criterion scores or rubric tables in transcript.

These simulate calendar pressure; follow them strictly.
